programme.desc: Writing To Make A Dent In The World Otani Akira, author of the Dagger Award-winning "The Night of Baba Yaga," discusses the inspirations behind her work and the complex themes she explores.
programme.title: Direct Talk

programme.desc: Spear Squid Michael gets on a boat with fishermen in Hokkaido and explores various Japanese and fusion dishes made with spear squid.
programme.title: Trails to Oishii Tokyo

programme.desc: Mother Nature's Summer Light Show The filmmakers examine what a group of women and children are looking for amongst the weeds near a stream on the outskirts of Edo.
programme.title: Ukiyoe Edo-Life

programme.desc: Betrayed by Forensics: Why DNA Analysis Was Manipulated The host sheds light on a case of forensic manipulation in Japan's police department, casting doubt on the credibility of criminal investigations.
programme.title: Today's Close-Up

programme.desc: Fuji Five Lakes: The Water of Life The filmmakers explore Mt Fuji and its five surrounding lakes in Yamanashi Prefecture.
programme.title: Seasoning the Seasons

programme.desc: Japan's disaster relief; Asia news; business and global current events.
programme.title: NHK Newsline

programme.desc: The Impact of Biologics on Inflammatory Diseases Experts investigate the potential and mechanisms of biologic drugs in treating chronic inflammatory diseases such as asthma and rheumatoid arthritis.
programme.title: Medical Frontiers

programme.desc: Japan's disaster relief; Asia news; business and global current events.
programme.title: NHK Newsline

programme.desc: Linear Rainbands Lemi Duncan sheds light on linear rainbands, a phenomenon which causes frequent torrential rain and floods in Japan, and why it is hard to make accurate forecasts.
programme.title: BOSAI: Science That Can Save Your Life

programme.desc: Food Waste Fuels Global Warming Food waste is a major driver of global warming; one institute warns that greenhouse gas emissions from food waste exceed those of nations like India and Russia; we examine the data behind this issue.
programme.title: IMPACTS:Climates Change the World

programme.desc: AMAMI: Shimauta: Amami's Soul Music Features shimauta, a traditional form of music that hails from the Amami Islands, an archipelago that was once part of the Ryukyu Kingdom in southern Japan.
programme.title: Time-Lapse Japan

programme.desc: Japan's disaster relief; Asia news; business and global current events.
programme.title: NHK Newsline

programme.desc: Eight Peter MacMillan discusses Japanese words related to the number eight and the unique culture surrounding it.
programme.title: Magical Japanese

programme.desc: I Can't Eat Raw Eggs. Fun and easy Japanese!; fast track your language learning with these simple expressions!; plus handy travel tips and easy kanji!; give it a try!
programme.title: Easy Japanese

programme.desc: A Children's Cafe with Foreign Cooks Vicky and his international friends prepare 100 servings of his signature dish, South Indian curry, for the International Children's Cafeteria in Matsuyama.
programme.title: Culture Crossroads

programme.desc: Children's Programs in English We introduce the English-language versions of two edutainment programs for kids; check out "The Wakey Show" with DJ Wakey and a cast of puppets and "I Love Mee," an animation exploring everyday questions.
programme.title: Hello! NHK WORLD-JAPAN

programme.desc: Japan's disaster relief; Asia news; business and global current events.
programme.title: NHK Newsline

programme.desc: Factory Groove : Wakayama's Textile Industry Explores Wakayama's textile production, the prefecture's staple industry, from colorful pile fabric to comfortable knits.
programme.title: Hometown Stories

programme.desc: Hot Days and Cool Nights Make Tasty Veggies Grandpa Makoto and Grandma Nobuko prepare easy-to-make, refreshing vegetable dishes to stay cool in the summer heat.
programme.title: Satoyama Kitchen Diary

programme.desc: Haneda to Fukuoka Airport Witness the virtual excursion from an airplane and take a look at the wonderful view during the flight to Fukuoka from an airplane window.
programme.title: On the Wings

programme.desc: Japan's disaster relief; Asia news; business and global current events.
programme.title: NHK Newsline

programme.desc: Another Kobe:A Thatched Roof Village Charles Glover explores rich nature, architecture, delicacies and a rural kabuki performance in Kita-ku, Kobe.
programme.title: Journeys in Japan

programme.desc: Japan's disaster relief; Asia news; business and global current events.
programme.title: NHK Newsline

programme.desc: Rika's Tokyo Cuisine/Omu-rice Hosts Yu Hayami and Patrick Harlan, with the help of Japanese chefs Tatsuo Saito and Rika Yukimasa, introduce Americans to the techniques, ingredients and harmony of Japanese cuisine.
programme.title: Dining with the Chef

programme.desc: Japan's disaster relief; Asia news; business and global current events.

Caesar the ape (Roddy McDowall) leads simians in revolt against the governor (Don Murray) enslaving them on future Earth.
programme.title: Conquest of the Planet of the Apes

programme.desc: The life of celebrated American writer Harry Street (Gregory Peck) flashes back before his eyes as he lies dying from a wound suffered at the foot of Tanzania's Mount Kilimanjaro.
programme.title: The Snows of Kilimanjaro

programme.desc: Two high-living adventurers settle in the world of fast cars, beautiful women and ruthless criminals.
programme.title: Mission: Monte Carlo

programme.desc: Flashbacks tell the story of a tenderfoot who rose to glory by gunning down the outlaw terrorizing his small town.
programme.title: The Man Who Shot Liberty Valance

programme.desc: An inventor's time machine takes Moe, Larry and Curly Joe to Ancient Greece.
programme.title: The Three Stooges Meet Hercules

programme.desc: A blind private eye (Edward Arnold) with a guide dog probes a murder for a friend (Ann Harding) and finds a Nazi spy ring.
programme.title: Eyes in the Night

programme.desc: Animators Max and Dave Fleischer's adaptation of Jonathan Swift's classic about a shipwrecked Englishman in Lilliput.
programme.title: Gulliver's Travels

programme.desc: An ex-convict bandit (Marlon Brando) finds the partner (Karl Malden) who betrayed him is now a town sheriff with a wife (Katy Jurado) and daughter.
programme.title: One-Eyed Jacks

programme.desc: A St. Louis lawyer's (Leon Ames) family (Judy Garland, Margaret O'Brien) stays in town for the 1904 World's Fair.
programme.title: Meet Me in St. Louis

programme.desc: Lovers (Lana Turner, Ezio Pinza) meet in Italy as an actress and a prince, then 12 years later as a movie star and a king.
programme.title: Mr. Imperium

programme.desc: Hopalong Cassidy (William Boyd) and his sidekicks (Russell Hayden, Andy Clyde) save a gold miner's daughter from a gambler.
programme.title: Pirates on Horseback

programme.desc: A framed sheriff (John McIntire) is about to hang, but his bogus wayward daughters have a plan.
programme.title: The New Daughters of Joshua Cabe

programme.desc: A folksy New England poet (Gary Cooper) inherits $20 million he doesn't want and tells a New York newswoman (Jean Arthur) why.
programme.title: Mr. Deeds Goes to Town

programme.desc: A reformed outlaw's (Elvis Presley) old gang tries to frame him for the theft of a jeweled Mexican cannon.
programme.title: Charro!

programme.desc: A Brooklyn bookkeeper (Don Knotts) falls off a pier, turns into a fish and helps the Navy track U-boats.
programme.title: The Incredible Mr. Limpet

programme.desc: Sitting in a wheelchair, his leg in a cast, a photographer (James Stewart) spies on courtyard neighbors and sees a murder.
programme.title: Rear Window

programme.desc: A San Francisco playgirl (Tippi Hedren) follows a bachelor (Rod Taylor) to Bodega Bay where, for no apparent reason, flocks of birds begin killing the populace.
programme.title: The Birds

programme.desc: After Herbert (Jerry Lewis) loses his lover, he gives up women for good. To get over them, he takes a maintenance position at boarding house, but to his surprise, the building caters specifically to single ladies.
programme.title: The Ladies' Man

programme.desc: An avenger's (Richard Harrison) quest in the name of his father leads from Mexico to a duel in Texas.
programme.title: Gunfight at Red Sands

programme.desc: Runyonesque Dave the Dude (Glenn Ford) turns Apple Annie (Bette Davis) into a Manhattan dowager in director Frank Capra's remake of his 1933 Lady for a Day.
programme.title: Pocketful of Miracles

programme.desc: Based on the story by Louisa May Alcott. The four March sisters forge unbreakable emotional ties during the Civil War.
programme.title: Little Women

programme.desc: A cattle baron (John Wayne) tries to tame his wife (Maureen O'Hara) amid a feud with settlers and trouble with Indians.
programme.title: McLintock!

programme.desc: Two vaudeville guys (Bing Crosby, Bob Hope) turn deep-sea divers and save a treasure belonging to a Polynesian princess (Dorothy Lamour).
programme.title: Road to Bali

programme.desc: Three retired Texas Rangers rescue a former colleague and eventually become the sheriff and deputies of Waco.
programme.title: The Over-the-Hill Gang Rides Again

programme.desc: In a Nazi prison barracks, other POWs believe that a cynical American sergeant (William Holden) is an informer.
programme.title: Stalag 17

programme.desc: A Harvard man (Robert Young) and his Colonial friend (Walter Brennan) join Rogers' (Spencer Tracy) Rangers in upstate New York.
programme.title: Northwest Passage

programme.desc: Three bumbling janitors encounter a slew of curious creatures after accidentally launching a rocket to Venus.
programme.title: Have Rocket, Will Travel

programme.desc: Lovers quarrel around surfers, Don Rickles, Buster Keaton, Von Zipper's biker gang.
programme.title: Beach Blanket Bingo

programme.desc: Supercomputer HAL 9000 guides astronauts (Keir Dullea, Gary Lockwood, William Sylvester) on a trip to find the origins of humans.
programme.title: 2001: A Space Odyssey

programme.desc: A beautiful detective goes undercover to investigate a huge drug ring in Los Angeles.
programme.title: Get Christie Love!

programme.desc: A murdered gangster makes a deal with the devil, agreeing to take over and corrupt a crusading judge's identity in return for avenging his murder.
programme.title: Angel on My Shoulder

programme.desc: The judge's (Lewis Stone) son (Mickey Rooney) goes to college after serving in the war and finds his sweetheart (Bonita Granville) is engaged to someone else.
programme.title: Love Laughs at Andy Hardy

programme.desc: Hopalong Cassidy (William Boyd) and his sidekicks (Russell Hayden, Andy Clyde) save a gold miner's daughter from a gambler.
programme.title: Pirates on Horseback

programme.desc: In the wake of the Portuguese Inquisition, on April 19, 1506, a three-day-long massacre led to the slaughter of 3,000 Jews in Lisbon. The fires shot as high as houses, in a city filled with quartered bodies and heads paraded on the points of spears.
